_comment: First-party tenant manifest — the declarative list of THIS operator's own
tenants. The rove engine has no built-in knowledge of these; this file +
the web/<dir> content dirs are the entire 'first-party-ness'. A different
operator deploying the engine drops in their own manifest + content and
runs the same driver (scripts/publish_firstparty.py) to stand up THEIR
first-party tenants. Nothing here is engine config.
Each tenant maps to one `rewind-ops` deploy: the source dir (under web/),
the tenant id it deploys to (note dir != id for system tenants — web/admin
deploys to __admin__, web/auth to __auth__), and the host(s) front routes
to it. Bundle classification (handlers vs _static/ vs _config/) is the
engine's, not this manifest's — see scripts/publish_tenant.py classify().
Fields per tenant:
tenant     required. tenant id the bundle deploys to.
dir        required. source dir relative to web/.
hosts      domains front routes to this tenant. hosts[0] is passed to
`provision --host`; the rest go through `host add`. A bare
{tenant}.rewindjs.app subdomain needs no entry (wildcard apex).
kind       'operator' (a real deployed site, published by default) or
'example' (a reference/template tenant; published only with
--include-examples or when named explicitly).
cluster    placement cluster. Omitted => defaults.cluster.
provision  create+place the tenant before deploy. Omit/true for normal
tenants; false for ones placed out-of-band (see __admin__).
release    flip _deploy/current live after staging. Omit => defaults.release.
note       free-text operator note (ignored by the driver).

tenants.note: OIDC IdP. _config/oidc/default.json mirrors to kv at release (config_mirror).
System tenant: placed once by `rewind-ops bootstrap` on a virgin cluster (NOT provisioned here). Ongoing publishes redeploy the dashboard/deploy app via the standing __admin__ workspace flow.
Reference browser-agent sample, not a production site. Reachable at agent-sample.rewindjs.app (wildcard) when published with --include-examples.
